runs.description: Transliterate the first available form; no branch balancing, no repairs (the original prototype behavior).
runs.name: baseline

runs.description: Branch-balanced skeleton vote + South-Slavic representative.
runs.name: +branch-consensus

runs.description: Six dialect-subgroup vote with population tie-break (§4.1).
runs.name: +six-subgroup

runs.description: Native POS lemma endings: noun nom.sg, adj -y/-i, verb -ti (§3).
runs.name: +lemma-endings

runs.description: Internationalism ending table: -izm/-cija/-ičny/-alny/-ovati (§5.2).
runs.name: +internationalism

runs.description: Normalize verbal/nominal prefixes råz-/prěd- (§2).
runs.name: +prefixes

runs.description: Undo East-Slavic pleophony / liquid metathesis (§2).
runs.name: +depleophony

runs.description: Recover ę/ų nasal vowels from Polish (§2 Phase C).
runs.name: +nasals

runs.description: Two-stage §4.4: consensus picks the root, the Proto-Slavic rule engine supplies the flavored form (ě/ć/đ/å/ȯ/y) via a leakage-free descendant+gloss link. Requires the proto cache.
runs.name: +proto-derived

runs.description: Prefer the internationalism cluster over native synonyms (ISV design criteria favor international roots for modern vocabulary): aeroplan over samolot.
runs.name: +intl-preference

runs.description: Drop a South-Slavic adjective's fleeting vowel before -y, gated on East/West consonant adjacency (dobar→dobry, zelen stays).
runs.name: +adj-fleeting

runs.description: Seed alternatives from secondary translations (below every primary candidate) so the official lemma surfaces in top-3/top-5 when it is a 2nd/3rd translation.
runs.name: +synonym-alts

runs.description: Grow proto-link coverage: strip a shared prefix off the cognates, link the bare root, re-attach the Interslavic prefix (råzprostirati from *prostirati).
runs.name: +prefix-strip

runs.description: Repair national adaptation quirks the representative leaks into a loan stem: Polish y→i, South-Slavic epenthetic vowel (akcenat→akcent), -ac→-ec, final -ia→-ija, masculine -a drop — each corroborated by a cognate or the internationalism gate.
runs.name: +loan-stem-repair

runs.description: Verb conjugation classes: jat after hushing spelled a (drzati, slysati), statives -eti on East/West e-stem evidence (kameneti).
runs.name: +verb-class

runs.description: Voicing correspondences: devoiced prefixes bes-/is- -> bez-/iz- and loan nz -> ns, each corroborated by a cognate with the voiced/Latin spelling.
runs.name: +voicing

runs.description: Use Wiktionary's stated (lang→ancestor) etymology to pick the Proto-Slavic reconstruction directly, before the fuzzy descendant+gloss link — the precise ancestor the corpus site uses.
runs.name: +explicit-etymology

runs.description: Pick the winning cluster's representative as the medoid — the member minimizing total folded edit distance to the others (the most central attested form) — instead of the fixed REP_PRIORITY, avoiding dialectal/oblique outliers. Measured by rep-eval (+1.09pp exact), the biggest recoverable slice of the +3.7pp oracle-representative ceiling.
runs.name: +medoid-rep

runs.description: Derivational-suffix normalization (root-consistency invariant [DERIV]), each categorical in the dictionary: -telj- kept before suffixes (53 -teljstvo/-teljny/-teljsky vs 0 hard), feminine i-stem soft -sť (516 vs 0), deverbal -livy (152 vs 0 -ljivy).
runs.name: +deriv-suffixes

runs.description: Keep the Graeco-Latin -ia-/-io- hiatus in internationalisms (socialny, entuziazm, sociolog) where the Slavic cognates' -ija- glide is a national adaptation: 24 -ial- vs 0 -ijal- in the dictionary, 139 midword -io- vs 1 -ijo-.
runs.name: +loan-hiatus

runs.description: Undo the *g→h spirantization a Czech/Slovak/Ukrainian/Belarusian representative leaks into the surface (blahosklonnost→blago-), corroborated per consonant position by ≥2 g-preserving cognates (ru/pl/South). ISV has no g→h rule (RULE_SPEC §2); genuine *x/loan h stays because the g-preserving lects write h there too.
runs.name: +spirantization

runs.description: Stem-class-aware citation endings (issue #76): a masculine n-stem's archaic nominative *-y survives the sound rules (*kamy→kamy) but the dictionary cites the extended oblique stem (kamenj, jęčmenj, plåmenj) — categorical in the official CSV. The Wiktionary declension category on the linked reconstruction supplies the class; link scoring stays stem_class-blind.
runs.name: +stem-class-endings (production)
